issues.title: HTTP: make timeout configurable; lower default from 300s to 60s
issues.body_raw:
**Complexity:** S
**Benefit:** Medium

## Problem

`timeout=300` is hardcoded in every HTTP method (`src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py:180,238,241,299,302,347`). Five-minute timeouts mask real problems: a hung call blocks bulk pipelines for ages before failing, and there is no way to dial it down for fast-fail cases or up for genuinely slow operations (large analytics reports).

## Proposal

- New constructor kwarg `timeout: float | None = 60.0`.
- Stored as `self.timeout`.
- Per-call override via the `_request(..., timeout=...)` parameter (lands cleanly with the verb-consolidation issue).
- CHANGELOG entry calling out the behavior change explicitly: "Default HTTP timeout reduced from 300s to 60s; pass `timeout=300` to AlmaAPIClient if you depend on the old default."

## Files to touch

- `src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py`
- `tests/unit/client/test_alma_api_client.py` (existing — add tests under)

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request() (cleaner per-call timeout once verbs are consolidated)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- Default lowered to 60s.
- `AlmaAPIClient(timeout=120.0)` accepted.
- Per-call override path exists.
- Test asserts the new default is in effect (e.g., monkeypatched session captures the timeout).
- CHANGELOG note added.
acceptance_criteria: Test asserts the new default is in effect (e.g., monkeypatched session captures the timeout).

issues.title: HTTP: make region/host configurable (currently EU is hardcoded)
issues.body_raw:
**Complexity:** S
**Benefit:** Medium

## Background

`self.base_url = "https://api-eu.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com"` is hardcoded at `src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py:93`. Anyone outside the EU must fork the package or monkey-patch the attribute. Trivial to add now, painful to add later once external callers depend on it being a constant.

A public downstream caller, [`Update_Alma_Digital_Collections`](https://github.com/hagaybar/Update_Alma_Digital_Collections), already has to disclose this constraint in its README — a real adoption blocker for any non-EU institution. Landing this issue lets that paragraph collapse into a single `region="NA"` kwarg in the example.

## Current wrapper behavior

`AlmaAPIClient._load_configuration()` hardcodes `self.base_url = "https://api-eu.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com"`. There is no constructor kwarg for region or host.

## Expected wrapper behavior

```python
REGION_HOSTS = {
    "EU":   "https://api-eu.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com",
    "NA":   "https://api-na.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com",
    "AP":   "https://api-ap.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com",      # Asia Pacific (Singapore)
    "APS":  "https://api-aps.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com",     # Asia Pacific (Australia)
    "CA":   "https://api-ca.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com",
    "CN":   "https://api-cn.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com.cn",   # China — note .com.cn TLD
}

def __init__(self, environment="SANDBOX", region="EU", host=None):
    ...
    if host is not None:
        self.base_url = host
    else:
        region = region.upper()
        if region not in REGION_HOSTS:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown region {region!r}; valid: {sorted(REGION_HOSTS)}")
        self.base_url = REGION_HOSTS[region]
```

## Implementation notes

- **Two distinct APAC hosts:** Ex Libris documents `api-ap.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com` (Singapore) and `api-aps.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com` (Australia) as separate datacenters. The codes must be distinct (`AP` vs `APS`); collapsing them into a single `APAC` entry is incorrect.
- **China uses `.com.cn`:** the documented host is `api-cn.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com.cn` (not `.com`). Easy to get wrong if you copy-paste from the other regions.
- Default region remains `"EU"` so existing callers see zero behavior change.
- The `host=` escape hatch supports proxies, mock servers in tests, and any future region the docs add before the registry is updated.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Default region remains `"EU"` — zero behavior change for existing callers.
- [ ] `AlmaAPIClient(region="NA")` works.
- [ ] Both `AlmaAPIClient(region="AP")` (Singapore) and `AlmaAPIClient(region="APS")` (Australia) resolve to their respective documented hosts.
- [ ] `AlmaAPIClient(region="CN")` resolves to `https://api-cn.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com.cn` (note `.com.cn` TLD).
- [ ] `AlmaAPIClient(host="https://api-eu.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com")` works (escape hatch for tests, proxies, or future regions).
- [ ] Invalid region raises `ValueError` listing valid options.
- [ ] `get_base_url()` / domain-knowledge sections of CLAUDE.md updated if relevant.

## Open questions

- None — Ex Libris's "Calling Alma APIs" page enumerates the host list explicitly.

## Audit notes (2026-05-01)

The original ticket listed five regions (`EU`, `NA`, `APAC`, `CA`, `CN`) with two errors flagged in both the structured audit and the Codex direct audit:
1. `APAC` collapsed two distinct documented datacenters (`api-ap` Singapore and `api-aps` Australia) into one entry.
2. `CN` used the `.com` TLD; the documented host is `.com.cn`.

This rewrite splits APAC into `AP` / `APS` and corrects the CN TLD per https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/.

## Files to touch

- `src/almaapitk/client/AlmaAPIClient.py`
- `tests/unit/client/test_alma_api_client.py` (existing — add tests under)

## Prerequisites

_None — this issue has no upstream dependencies and does not have recommended soft prereqs at the toolkit level._
acceptance_criteria: Default region remains `"EU"` — zero behavior change for existing callers.
Both `AlmaAPIClient(region="AP")` (Singapore) and `AlmaAPIClient(region="APS")` (Australia) resolve to their respective documented hosts.
`AlmaAPIClient(host="https://api-eu.hosted.exlibrisgroup.com")` works (escape hatch for tests, proxies, or future regions).
Invalid region raises `ValueError` listing valid options.
`get_base_url()` / domain-knowledge sections of CLAUDE.md updated if relevant.
